Just Cause 3 is what happens when a game stops pretending to be realistic and fully commits to explosions, wingsuits, grappling hooks, chaos, and ridiculous fun. The story is not the point. The point is launching yourself across the map, destroying half a military base, and somehow surviving because the game understands that fun matters more than logic.

Verdict: Recommended for pure chaos.

Nothing quite beats wingsuiting into a cow then immediately exploding.

A game about pure dumb fun, perfection.

More than a decade after its release, this game remains nothing short of incredible. Even in 2026, it continues to impress with its quality, gameplay, and overall experience. It's genuinely surprising how well it has aged compared to many newer titles.
The only real drawback is the story mode, which can become quite repetitive over time. However, aside from that, the game excels in almost every area and remains highly enjoyable even years later.
If you're considering giving it a try, I can confidently recommend it. Few games manage to stay this entertaining and relevant more than ten years after launch, and this is definitely one of them.

[h1] Fighting To Save His Home... [/h1]

[b] The Positive's [/b]
+ Big Destructible Playground  
+ Lots Of Vehicles 
+ Decent Story

[b] The Negative's [/b]
- Collectible Central 
- Minor Bugs

[b] The Aspect Of The Game [/b]
[i] You play as Agency dictator removal specialist Rico Rodriguez returning to his homeland the republic of Medici which is a Mediterranean island paradise ran by dictator  General Di Ravello an insane man that needs to removed from power. Aid in the rebellion and cause as much chaos as possible! [/i]

[b] My Two Cent Review [/b]
[i] I've played the first Just Cause back in 2013 then Just Cause 2 in 2018 and now I've finally gotten around to playing Just Cause 3 in 2026... the gaming well is running dry for modern games so I decided it's time to continue the series of Just Cause with playing this installment. The main game itself isn't bad though it's story is a little dry but the map makes up for it being a giant playground of destruction. I got to play with just about every vehicle known to man and even Mechs which was cool to use. I can't walk a mile down road however without running into one of many many collectibles plaguing the map which I didn't think Ubisoft had a part in making this game... As for the stability of the game it still runs well with the occasional mission bug where enemies don't spawn in or your ally's vehicle just randomly blowing up out of nowhere from time to nothing but nothing a simple reload last checkpoint can't fix. I did enjoy taking over the islands slowly and there certainly was a lot to take over with there being major bases, to small outpost, to towns & cities I destroyed and took over all. This game wasn't as thrilling as Just Cause 2 I had to drop the game and come back to it after awhile but the important thing is did beat it. Don't think i'll be coming back to it but it was a fun time waster. [/i]

[b] The Value Of The Game [/b]
[i] Games about worth the 3.99$ price of it goes on sale for every steam sale. It's slightly worse then 2 but probably the last good Just Cause game in the series since the things i'm hearing about 4 are terribly awful... can't wait to play that slop myself. [/i]

[h1] Final Score 79/100 Ranking C+ Tier [/h1]

well i dont know where to stary but lets just say that Just Cause 3 is pure chaos in the best possible way flying through the air with the wingsuit blowing up military bases and causing massive explosions never gets old gotta say that graphics are amazing i forgot how beautiful this game can be at some times especially the beautiful Mediterranean inspired world sunsets and destruction effects the game doesnt take itself too seriously and thats exactly why its so much fun 10/10  absolute cinema!

Pure chaos in the best way possible. Wingsuiting across the map, chaining explosions together, and tethering random stuff to helicopters never stops being fun. The story is pretty forgettable, but nobody’s here for the story anyway. This game fully commits to being a sandbox for destruction, and it absolutely delivers.
This game was amazing and a perfect balance between peace and chaos. It's a shame that Just Cause 4 was bad and led to the downfall of the Just Cause series.
Very fun gameplay, action-packed and vastly big open world to use your gadgets on. And Rico is a badass.
bought this at the price of 59 INR. so worth it. so beautfiul and fun game. i wish i could erase my memories and re play this game. oh my days 10/10.

I have this on the ps4 and have played it to death so when I saw it on sale here for less than £2 I had to get it. This is such a fun game, you don't need to think too hard about things, all you need to do is cause chaos and liberate all the towns and islands. There is something just so much fun watching chains of explosions and the chaos and destruction they cause. Feeling a little bored, go cause chaos and destroy stuff, had a bad day, go cause chaos and destroy stuff.
Os graficos são sensacionais, historia até que e boa. Dialogos meio travados. Porém o jogo entrega oque realmente se pede... Fianl bom e desenvolvimento de personagens bom também.
This game is very good actually game of my childhood,Its fun and u dont need a NASA pc to run it.Good game after all,

this game is like a cheesy action movie but better. If you expect it to be bad, it'll be a masterpiece. If you think it'll be a masterpiece, it'll be bad. Don't play with high hopes, but it is great if you don't want a masterpiece of a game and instead just a game to have fum.

This shi was incredibly fun game with over-the-top explosions and pure sandbox chaos. The traversal system is one of the most unique parts of the game and you can use almost any vehicle you find, and on top of that you have a parachute, wingsuit, and grappling hook.

Honestly, it feels like being a "Supaidaman", flying around with cables and pulling off ridiculous stunts everywhere. The over-the-top action makes everything more entertaining, and it's easy to spend hours just causing chaos instead of following the main story.

The vehicle controls took me some time to get used to, especially for aircraft and certain land vehicles, since they feel a bit different from other games. However, once I adapted to them, traveling around the map became a lot more enjoyable.

The combat system was also a little confusing at first because of the controls and mechanics, but after a while it became second nature. Once everything clicks, the game becomes even more fun thanks to its explosive and over-the-top gameplay
Still a great sandbox. Donnt bother with JC4, get this instead. Been playing this for a few years, yes a few years, and it never gets old.

Just Cause 2 was fun, don't get me wrong. But this game... this game is the peak. Don't even waste your time with Just Cause 4 and its weather nonsense because it's absolute trash.

Do you want to glide around liberating towns and destroying enemy strongholds? This is the game for you.

Do you want to pilot military jets, only to jump out at the last second and glide down to safety while taking out a platoon of grunts? Then, well, this is the game for you.

This game knows what it is and not only that it embraces it. This game asks "How much destruction do you want?" and then somehow offers more.

I don't know what happened to Avalanche Studios after this game was released, but, this... this is their magnum opus.
Destruction is awesome. Movement is awesome. Story and mission gameplay is weaker but still fun.

This game understands one very important thing: explosions are better when you arrive by wingsuit, leave by parachute, and have no reasonable explanation for what happened in between.

The movement is easily the best part. The grappling hook, parachute, and wingsuit combo makes just getting across the map fun, which is rare for an open-world game. You’re not just traveling from one icon to another — you’re launching yourself off cliffs, slingshotting across roads, diving between mountains, and somehow turning basic transportation into a personal stunt show.

The destruction is also exactly what I want from this series. Bases, fuel tanks, towers, bridges, vehicles — everything feels like it was placed there specifically so Rico could look at it and say, “Yeah, that probably shouldn’t still be standing.” Liberating towns and military bases can get chaotic fast, but that’s the whole point. The plan is usually optional. The explosions are not.

It’s beautiful, ridiculous, loud, and very good at making you feel like the world’s most irresponsible action hero. Not a deep emotional journey, unless you count forming a bond with a gas tank seconds before it becomes a fireworks display. Very fun game.

Look, I know I havn't really played that much. But O MY GOODNESS... Like fr this game is actually one of the most fun games to mess around with. The graphics are much better than you'd think, but the main thing is the physics is just satisfying. Who cares how realistic it looks when it makes my dompamine recepters go insane.

And a lot of people might tell you that the story is kind of dead, DONT LISTEN TO THEM. Like legitemately I don't see how anyone could complain.

Also this game totally holds up even today, and proabbly the best just cause game overall.

The App says ive played this game for 12 hours.... but thats literally me playing the first missions over and over trying to find a way to save the game. ive re downloaded this game so many times idek, on different drives... as admin! idk anymore... 12 hours and am still on the first mission wow

and i see more people getting this issue online with this game.... sad

Failed physics 2 so breaking the laws of physics in this game makes me feel a bit better 

Get the Jc3 unlimited mod either you're a old/ returning or new player it makes the game even better with its QoL changes

Okay idk what it is but for some dumbass reason this game literally CANNOT perform on my PC which is surprising. I go from 200 fps down to 5 up to 30 down to 5 then back up to 200 every few seconds making the game completely unplayable. I've looked up things to do and I've done them, they've helped, but still has performance issues. IT sucks because I've played JC2-JC4 on my Xbox with absolutely no issues. I heard it comes down to how the game utilizes modern day hardware and that it usually is the main culprit for the game not working. I love this game but I'm very disappointed there has never been an update to resolve these issues, because now I can't play one of my favorite games ever.
Childhood game still insanely fun and alot of things to do
I am a fan of games which allow military open world where one army is against another and you take out enemy bases and establish control. This game is perfect for you. Love this game a lot.
Best game of my childhood and my favorite game to explode everything.
Simply the best version of the series, that I keep coming back even thought it is over 10 years old. It's a classic and for my money the best open-world sand-box action game of all time.
fun sandbox I love the hook mechanic and taking out military bases by blowing a bunch of stuff up I wouldn't get this game for purely a good story more just a fun watered down gta5
amazing gameplay,amazing graphics,amazing story. the only thing i can complaing is the car camera and that we can't aim while shooting except for the sniper
Amazing game. Great gameplay, graphics, story, everything. Highly recommended.
when im travelling at a velocity that would normally turn my lower half into jelly but i grapple down so its ok

Beyond my expectations!

I always wanted to play this game but never really gave it a try. Now that I finally have more than 10 hours into it, I have to say it is absolutely beyond my expectations! It is incredibly fun, highly addictive, and packed with so many options and little details.

The open-world freedom in this game is amazing!! There is just so much to do and explore, and the sheer scale of the world is impressive. If you love massive open worlds where you can just cause absolute chaos and experiment with different mechanics, this is it. I'm completely hooked and loving every minute of it!
got for a discount never heard of it before . Best game i have ever played destruction is very good. You will have many ways to clear a province so it will not feel repetitive at all.

Honestly, a good time! The grappling hook and the gliding vest work perfectly for traversal, and you'll find yourself wanting to just glide around. The traversal is on par with Batman Arkham games and Spider-man games. Also, explosions!
My favorite story game of all time. I'm not one to replay games, let alone story games, but I've replayed JC3 multiple times.

i love the game, although the intro was annoying to sit through, its only like 5 or more sec long but its everytime you open the game, also one thing i would of liked being changed is that the final mission, i didnt realise it was the final mission because i expected you had to take over all the places, i would of liked it to be that way but all the dlcs and stuff was amazing its a great game sometimes it would crash but i think that was just bc of my pc specs

This is one of the most fun games I've ever played on a computer.  It was the first game i purchased when i built my own PC back in 2016 and it still holds up - the movement is incredible and the gameplay loop is addicting.  Cannot recommend this game enough
Extremely fun, the story is pretty good, It's a solid game with a good world. 8.5/10
fun, exploring, destruction, chaos, stunts and everythung else that you can think of
One of the best games i played. I read this was called better than JC4 by alot of them. IMO world is so beautiful they should have just made dlc for JC4 story. Game is dirt cheap too.

Even though the world isn't fully interactive and the story is simple, the gameplay is so incredibly fun that it's absolutely worth playing!

Rating: 3/5 .

I had this game for a while in my library, I jumped on for an hour or so but would come off it. Looking back I don't know why, this game is amazing for either trying to complete everything it has to offer or just turning your brain off and shooting/blowing everything up without any repercussions to the story. lots of guns, cars, planes and creative ways to blow stuff up. Throw the grappling hook in on top of it. You wont even need to drive half of the time if you want to fly around with a wing suit. and the map is HUGE.

The story is good, DLC is alright also.

Its definitely worth the £12. But if you want to, wait for a sale and buy the DLC to experience everything this game has to offer.
Very good game, things i don't like however is repition and how you can't change the difficulty. Also the ai is very nrealistic
really really really addicting at first, but kinda repetitive after dozens of playtime

The ultimate "Michael Bay" simulator that turns the laws of physics into a mere suggestion.

Just Cause 3 is a rare masterpiece of pure, unadulterated chaos that feels just as satisfying to play today as it did a decade ago. It is the definitive sandbox for anyone who thinks "rational gameplay" is overrated and "giant explosions" are a personality trait. The island of Medici is a gorgeous, sun-drenched playground that exists for one reason: for you to tear it apart. Between the tethering system that lets you slingshot tanks into helicopters and the most satisfying destruction physics in gaming history, the creative possibilities for mayhem are endless. It doesn't ask you to be a hero; it asks you to be a one-man demolition crew with a death wish and a bottomless supply of C4.

The absolute crown jewel of this game is the wingsuit. While Just Cause 4 tried to add more weather-based gimmicks, it never quite captured the perfect, fluid "falling with style" feel of the third entry. Traversal isn't just a way to get to the mission; it is the mission. Combining the grapple, the parachute, and the wingsuit into a seamless loop of high-speed flight is so addictive that you’ll find yourself ignoring the map markers just to see how long you can glide through the Mediterranean hills without touching the ground. If you have the Sky Fortress DLC, the jet-powered Bavarian Wingsuit essentially turns Rico Rodriguez into Iron Man, making the game feel like a completely different (and even more ridiculous) superhero simulator.

Despite its legendary status, Just Cause 3 is still a bit of a "diva" when it comes to performance. Even on high-end 2026 hardware, you might encounter the occasional stutter or memory leak, and the "always-online" leaderboard system can still be a nuisance if you just want to blow stuff up in peace. The driving mechanics remain notoriously "wonky," and the story is about as deep as a puddle—but let's be honest, you aren't here for the character arcs. You're here to see how many fighter jets you can tether together before they explode. It’s a loud, silly, and incredibly fun "no-brainer" game that proves that sometimes, the best way to save the world is to blow up every gas station in sight.

10/10 — I tethered a dictator to a gas canister and sent him to the moon. 10/10 liberation.
it was a fun fun game, i loved playing it.... beautiful map and amazing gameplay and nice cars and guns and grappling and wingsuit are awesome to control ... loved it :-)
first just cause that i played and gotta say i love the action on this one. Story seems good, graphics are good and the gameplay is great
Good game i was playing in 720p but game was good best story if you get this for less then 60 RS (Indian) rs then get this game

This game is great, the story is pretty simple, but the gameplay loop is ridiculously fun an addictive. If you enjoy blowing stuff up or driving vehicles, then this game is for you. I also recommend waiting for the game to go on sale because I was able to get the complete edition for about five (U.S.) dollars
Incredibly fun and addictive, never gets old! I replay it from time to time

played this years ago when it was new and glad to say it still holds up, runs great (on my linux system too!)

quite different from just cause 2 but still very enjoyable, and probably the best game in the series, i'd say just cause 2 is still brilliant but i just find this more fun to actually play

massively worth it espcially on sale
Never played other games in series, but i really liked this game. It's literally "chaotically fun".
really really good, but why is the game 60 gigs and why is it so buggy
five stars so good amazing runs smoothly on not hiogh performin devices and is like a gta for teens
conquering outposts like FarCry, while swinging around like Spidey. This game is a lot of fun. the driving is not great (especially the motorcycles) but why drive when you can fly?
Peak Fun. Not much story tho so dont buy the game for that cause its all Destruction. All 3 Expansions are worth it especially Air. Scouring the map for secrets was also fun

This is one of my favorite single player games of all time. Everything about it is great from the gameplay to the gunplay, to the physics, to the wingsuit and the grappling hook. it's a wonderful time and I highly recommend it. My only complaint is that it doesn't have steam cloud support. i would have 100% this game and got all the achievements if i did not need to restart every time I reinstall it.

Why are you conquering an island? Just Cause I can. Peak game, better than the 4th part, tbh this is the best part hands down. I reccomend buying the dlcs, lots of fun and stuff to discover, I don't regret playing this game even a bit.
10/10

I liked the game a lot, although it definitely has its own flaws.

Gameplay +++
Even if you have to capture quite a few outposts, the variety of weapons and vehicles makes it fun. Seriously, try destroying an outpost with a plane or a helicopter at least once.

Atmosphere +++
The atmosphere is amazing. I really enjoyed simply exploring the world. The different biomes and economic regions show the dictator’s country from many different perspectives.

Story -
The story is very straightforward and predictable. If you’re looking for a deep, story-rich experience, I probably wouldn’t recommend this game for that.

Overall, if you just want to spend some time fighting a dictator with a huge arsenal of weapons and vehicles — I highly recommend it. You’ll definitely have fun.
just cause 3 is good and optamized confort for gamers want violence

Boss just play it either pay the $20 or get it on sale you won't waste the money if you like open world games, main game is fun and the DLC makes it even better can't recommend it enough this game truly is the best in the series and is a master piece from the studio.

I have only played a few hrs rn but I still love this game and it's mechanics it's just so much much with the innovate grappling mechanic compared to other similar games in this genre. 

Story is kinda mid but I can't help but notice how close this story is to real life 'rebel' groups in the middle east toppling dictators like Gaddafi or Saddam or more recently Assad. The rebels appear to be 'better' for the country but in aftermath of the civil war the common man is almost always worse off. Even in this game we just mercilessly destroy literally all of the infrastructure on that little island how tf are these mfs gonna live when we're done 'liberating' the island? Similarly after 'liberating' cities I can kill as many civilians as I want in them while in 'oppressed' cities the dictator's forces atleast try to stop me from killing civilians. So really are the people of Medici better under the rebels or their old dictator?

Anyways coming back to the game I really have enjoyed it so far however the 'save' mechanic could use some work like let us save manually instead of relying on autosaves. But yeah otherwise this game is good fun with lots of interesting stuff to do, very easy to run and definitely worth it's small price tag.

Complete-♥♥♥♥♥♥♥-ly broken. Game doesnt even launch for me anymore because it has to open through steam which just doesnt work anymore. I shouldnt have to pirate a game that I already own in order to actually be able to play it.

An action sandbox game that doesn't do much to shake up it's gameplay in a way that interested me in the opening hours. You quickly realize the majority of your objectives will be clearing out repetitive camps with the same objectives repeatedly. What really blows is the car controls being absolute boats when their previous game, Mad Max, had great ones. It's fun as a shut off your brain game, but when there's nothing else going on it doesn't make for an engaging experience.

i meet all the recommended requirements but still this runs like on 25 fps om low settings AAA games like RDR2 and GTAV runs better while still being on medium to high setting WHAT A TRASH OPTIMISATION THIS GAME HAS!!!!

The game's alright.  It's a decent sandbox, but I'm not a fan of the actual missions.

I'm not recommending it because it crashes quite a bit.  More than any game I've played in years.

I really wish to like this game, cause i LOVED just cause 4, but for some reason, the game just doesn't hit me, really out of objetives, fells like a sandbox experience when you want to destroy things, but worst than jc4. 

Not saying that jc4 is COMPLETELY worst, it is flawed, but the lack of story in this game completely break the vibe. the fact that your objective IS to destruct the bases, and the exact same thing for the main missions, just makes you question why the ♥♥♥♥ your doing that, and why you are playing something that LITERALLY won't change nothing in your life

BOOM! 💥 woooshhhhh ..... Wooooshhhh BOOM 💥 BOOM 💥 BOOM! 💥 I am using the DLC wingsuit upgrade btw. Brrrttttt Brrrttt BOOM BOOM BOOM 💥 

I honestly think I have destroyed the city more than the antagonist did. I am just Chaos! I attach people to the stationary tall crane with a long line and then I attach 5 sticky bombs with the max booster upgrade and then I reel the line and let them go then I activate the bombs and watch them launch like a trebuchet then into ROCKETS! 

The only thing I hate about this game is how the camera works when I am in a vehicle and I am using controllers, the camera moves so wonky and especially when I am in a  vehicle that can shoot. 

I love this game. Played it before without the DLCs but getting the wing suit upgrade DLC makes traversal much much easier. I no longer get stressed out when I am panicking when a lot of enemies are shooting at me because with the jetpack wing suit, I could just fly away from my problems. 

Actually I have one more gripe, WHY THE HECK ARE THE GUNNERD ON THE BOAT SO DAMN ACCURATE?! Then even still hit me when I am air gliding like they are snipers but with LMGs.

And I thought Just Cause 4 was more trash, but it turned out that Just Cause 3 was no less trash.
Too few pluses compared to 4 but not enough for the game to not be garbage.
A small part of the things are useful, everything else is completely useless and the way to acquire them is even worse than 4.
Lazily made garbage.

Would be a fun game, but the bugs break entire missions. For example, enemies glitch inside walls when the mission goals are to clear the area of enemies. And you can't destroy the walls/buildings anyways. Game has promise, but the bugs aren't worth it. Glad I only wasted ~5 bucks on sale. Not recommended.

Having loved JC2, I really wanted to like this game too. But I can't. The unskippable intro before even starting to load the game was not setting a very good mood every time I tried. It went only downhill from there. No matter what PC specs I have tried, the game is laggy and unpleasant, any movement feels like the character is drunk all the time.
low budget spiderman with badass explosions and machine guns

Just Cause 3 looks exciting at first but falls flat quickly. The story is weak and generic with forgettable characters. It's full of glitches that ruin the fun, and the technical issues are terrible — constant frame drops, stuttering, and long loading times. The graphics look dated too.
Worst of all, the vehicle handling feels horrible and floaty, making the gameplay frustrating instead of enjoyable.
Overall, a buggy and repetitive mess that gets old very fast.
All i have to say its a nice game but practically its copy of far cry concept but lower version i know its a old game but buy this game on your own risk you cant aim though
Hello, my name is Rico, i tether old lady's onto cars and watch there wrinkly skin disintegrate from getting dragged onto hot pavement. I also make stuff go boom.
exsplosions over throwing a dictatior and more exspolsions
Destraction.Chaos.Extreme Espaces, and fast cars. What else needeed? :)

I just have not had the chance to settle in and play this one like I did 2 but from what little I have played of 3, I have enjoyed every bit of it. The only struggle I had so far was trying to figure out where to go next in the Main Quest. So, I just started going around causing the same mayhem as I did in 2 and things got really fun then.
Perfect for people that like destruction & explosions
didn't think you could live up to the hype of a jc2 follow up but they kept the formula upped the visuals and added more content. love it
This is basically destruction similator but with ememies and super weapons. Makes you basically feel like a Super hero that took destruction to a whole new level.

Very Amazing game, slightly laggy for my PC so I have to use a low resolution of 1280x720, Still kind of laggy but not that bad. Driving with navigation on feels kind of like Forza, the open world concept is really nice. Overall a great game.

I would highly recommend this game. Although it can be a bit clunky at some times, it is one of the best open world games of all time. If you like games where you can do basically anything you want, then this game is for you. It has hours upon hours of extra things to do other than the main story and is overall very rewarding and fun. The main story is a bit boring in my opinion and isn't all important to me but it definitely isn't unfun to play. This game has some of the most replayability of any game I've ever played and for that fact i cant give it anything other than a 10/10.

This game has some neat ideas, but poor design pointless annoyances just keep getting in the way.

Zipping around with the grappling hook/parachute/wingsuit combo can be fun, but the controls are kind of clunky and there are a lot of ragdoll physic and bad AI. For a game that relies so much on vehicles and stunts, the driving controls are surprisingly bad, and it takes almost nothing to send your vehicle careening off the road doing cartwheels. Other vehicles don't even seem to be aware of you until they hit you, since you can be sitting still and they will just rear end you.

That goes for the combat as well. There is no health bar, you can take an absurd amount of damage, and heal really fast, which helps add to the whole over the top action movie vibe they are going for. It also makes combat possible, because the game would be impossible without it. The shooting is a bit janky, and some of the enemies are bullet sponges. And the AI goes in between braindead and aimbot. On the ground, the regular guys might as well be stormtroopers with how bad they aim, but the moment you get in the air, all the sudden everyone is a rocket launcher wielding sniper. When you are trying to take over a base, enemies just continuously spawn right on top of you. The spawn rules seem to be "if the camera isn't pointed in that direction, enemies can spawn there" because I would find myself clearing an area with no ingress, only to turn my head 90 degrees and have half a dozen guys spawn right outside my periphery.

Another major annoyance is that despite being a single-player game, the game automatically logs you into a server when you launch, presumably to track some leaderboards that no one cares about. This causes issues because you will keep getting disconnected, which gives you a pop-up that interrupts game play. This happens fairly often. In the popup, you have the option to try to reconnect or go into offline  mode, but even in offline mode it still connects any time you start a challenge, which is the core of the skill progression, or even open the map/menu. So, you try to open your map and you get interrupted while it connects to a server.
